reasoning: Merged 5 patches covering spreadsheet manipulation best practices. Key convergences identified: openpyxl cell-level preference (4/5 patches), pre-modification inspection (3/5 patches), validation/logging (3/5 patches), and explicit range handling (4/5 patches). Conflicts resolved by keeping strongest justifications - e.g., copy-before-modify pattern from Patch 1 takes priority, while reverse-order deletion from Patch 3 is preserved as essential. Removed all benchmark-specific IDs, cell references, and concrete constants per filtering rules. Consolidated overlapping concepts like 'lookup dictionaries' and 'hash tracking' into unified patterns. Merged edit content while eliminating redundant headings.

memory_items.title: Workbook Exploration Protocol
memory_items.lesson: Always inspect available sheets and their schemas before planning transformations
memory_items.generalization: Load workbook metadata, enumerate sheet names, and preview relevant worksheets' columns and row counts to inform downstream logic

memory_items.title: Output Workbook Isolation
memory_items.lesson: Copy source workbook to output path before making modifications
memory_items.generalization: Preserve original file integrity using copy operation first; write all changes only to the isolated output copy

memory_items.title: Direct Cell-Level Access
memory_items.lesson: Use openpyxl cell-level operations instead of pandas when precise control is needed
memory_items.generalization: Iterate through result values with nested loops, setting cells explicitly for full range and formatting control

memory_items.title: Dynamic Range Detection
memory_items.lesson: Detect data boundaries at runtime instead of assuming fixed indices
memory_items.generalization: Use workspace properties like max_row and max_column to iterate over actual data ranges

memory_items.title: Identifier vs Value Separation
memory_items.lesson: Separate stable identifier columns from transposable value columns programmatically
memory_items.generalization: Maintain configurable list of identifier columns; derive remaining columns dynamically

memory_items.title: Pre-Computed Lookup Maps
memory_items.lesson: Build in-memory dictionaries from reference tables before main transformation loop
memory_items.generalization: Convert lookup sheets to key-value mappings for O(1) access during row-wise iteration

memory_items.title: Cross-Source Validation
memory_items.lesson: Verify that keys in source intersect properly with keys in lookup table
memory_items.generalization: Filter valid variables before processing to prevent missing-key errors

memory_items.title: Clean Target Sheet Replacement
memory_items.lesson: Delete existing target sheet before recreating to avoid duplicate rows
memory_items.generalization: Check if target sheet exists; remove it before creating new sheet for clean output

memory_items.title: Reverse-Order Row Deletion
memory_items.lesson: When deleting multiple rows, process highest indices first
memory_items.generalization: Row removal affecting multiple rows must sort indices descending; otherwise lower rows shift after each deletion

memory_items.title: Two-Pass Batch Operations
memory_items.lesson: Separate data scanning from destructive actions when modifying multiple rows
memory_items.generalization: First pass collects information about matching rows; second pass executes changes for deterministic results

memory_items.title: Column Index Mapping
memory_items.lesson: Verify Excel column letter to integer index mapping before range operations
memory_items.generalization: Convert column letters to consistent integer positions programmatically rather than hardcoding assumptions

memory_items.title: Circular Reference Prevention
memory_items.lesson: Check if formula includes its own position in referenced ranges
memory_items.generalization: For aggregates over ranges, split into comma-separated sub-ranges excluding target cell if needed

memory_items.title: Formula Storage in OpenPyXL
memory_items.lesson: Store formulas as strings via cell.value assignment
memory_items.generalization: Some Cell attributes may not exist; use safe property access patterns for reading/writing formula content

memory_items.title: Global Styling First Pattern
memory_items.lesson: Apply universal formatting before targeted value changes
memory_items.generalization: Complete all sheet-wide style operations before data transformations for consistent baseline

memory_items.title: Threshold-Based Value Distribution
memory_items.lesson: For capping values, calculate excess and write to adjacent column
memory_items.generalization: Store capped value in source cell; compute overflow (original minus cap) for destination column

memory_items.title: Type-Sensitive Number Formatting
memory_items.lesson: Detect integer vs decimal values and apply appropriate format codes
memory_items.generalization: Check numeric type properties before applying number_format to preserve clean display

memory_items.title: Robust Numeric Conversion
memory_items.lesson: Wrap value-to-number conversions in exception handlers
memory_items.generalization: Always use try/except blocks around float/int conversions to handle malformed data gracefully

memory_items.title: Null Safety During Iteration
memory_items.lesson: Validate cell contents against None/null before processing
memory_items.generalization: Include explicit null checks when iterating cell values to skip empty cells without errors

memory_items.title: Transformation Verification Logging
memory_items.lesson: Print intermediate metrics during transformation for debugging
memory_items.generalization: Log DataFrame shapes, row counts, and sample outputs before/after critical operations

edits.content:
## Trace-Derived Lessons

### Foundational Workflow

**Workbook Exploration Protocol:** Before transformation, list all sheet names and preview column schemas using openpyxl and pandas. This identifies identifier vs transposable columns and validates cross-sheet dependencies.

**Output Workbook Isolation:** Always copy source workbook to output path before modifications. Write changes only to the isolated copy to preserve original file integrity.

**Target Sheet Replacement:** When updating an existing sheet, delete it (`del wb['sheetname']`) before `wb.create_sheet()` to avoid duplicate rows or orphaned data.

### Data Manipulation Patterns

**Direct Cell-Level Access:** Use openpyxl cell objects (`ws.cell(row=X, column=Y)`) instead of pandas when precise positioning or structure preservation is required. Iterate through values with nested loops for full range control.

**Dynamic Range Detection:** Determine iteration bounds via `ws.max_row` and `ws.max_column` properties at runtime rather than hardcoded ranges.

**Identifier vs Value Separation:** Define identifier columns explicitly; derive remaining columns programmatically using list comprehensions for flexible reshaping.

**Pre-Computed Lookup Maps:** Convert reference sheets to in-memory dictionaries using key-value mappings for O(1) access during row-wise iteration, reducing repeated lookups.

**Cross-Source Validation:** Filter source columns against lookup table keys before processing using membership checks to catch mismatches early.

**Hash-Based Deduplication:** For assigning sequential identifiers based on distinct values, maintain an in-memory dictionary mapping values to their assigned IDs for consistent assignments.

### Multi-Row Operations

**Reverse-Order Deletion:** When deleting multiple rows, always sort target row indices in DESCENDING order before iteration. Processing bottom-to-top prevents index shifting errors.

**Scan Before Action:** Collect all candidate rows matching criteria first, store in a list, then execute deletions in separate loop for deterministic results.

**Column Index Mapping:** Verify Excel column letters map to correct integer indices consistently throughout range checks rather than assuming hardcoded positions.

### Formula Management

**Self-Reference Detection:** Check if a formula's referenced range includes its own position. Aggregates over self-containing ranges cause circular dependency errors.

**Range Exclusion Strategy:** To exclude target cell from continuous range, split into two comma-separated sub-ranges (before and after target row).

**Formula Storage:** Store formulas as strings via cell.value assignment. Some Cell attributes may not exist—use safe property access patterns.

### Formatting and Validation

**Global Styling First:** Complete all sheet-wide style operations (fonts, colors, borders) before targeted data transformations.

**Threshold-Based Value Distribution:** For value caps, store capped value in source cell; compute overflow (original minus threshold) and place in adjacent column.

**Type-Sensitive Number Formatting:** Check if numeric value is integer before applying number_format codes to preserve clean display of whole numbers while maintaining decimal precision.

**Robust Type Conversions:** Wrap all float()/int() conversions in try/except blocks. Non-numeric cells should be skipped gracefully without breaking execution.

**Null Safety:** Include explicit None/value checks when iterating cell contents to handle empty cells without runtime exceptions.

### Verification and Logging

**Pre-Modification Diagnostics:** Inspect target cells, data types, and formula integrity before implementing changes to validate assumptions.

**Status Reporting:** Log processing statistics (rows checked, rows deleted/kept, unique counts, samples) to enable post-execution verification.

**Diagnostic Checks:** Count numeric/non-numeric entries in ranges, verify expected calculations match observations before applying formula changes.

changelog_entries: Consolidated 5 patches into unified spreadsheet manipulation guidelines
Merged overlapping concepts: workbook exploration, openpyxl preferences, logging/validation
Added reverse-order row deletion and batch operation patterns from Patch 3
Integrated circular reference detection and formula storage patterns from Patch 4
Combined dynamic range detection and hash-based deduplication from Patch 5
Removed all benchmark-specific IDs, cell references, and concrete constants
Standardized formatting with clear section headers and bullet points
Prioritized prevalent patterns appearing in 3+ independent patches
